titleStmt.title: Letter from Dermot MacDermot to Edward O'Farrell, 19 June 1916.
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Dermot MacDermot

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Dermot MacDermot to Edward O'Farrell (d.1926), Assistant Under-Secretary, Dublin Castle asking to be considered for the vacant post of Clerk of the Crown and Peace for County Westmeath.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5615/10367/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Boyle Co Roscommon. June 19th. 1916 Sir I have seen the announcement
of the death of Robert Kelly, who
held the post of Clerkship of the Crown
and Peace for Co Westmeath.
I beg to apply through you for the
vacancy, and will be obliged if you
will forward my application to the
proper quarters. I have had an
extensive County Court and Equity
practice for the past thirteen years
and hold the highest testimonials as
to my efficiency. I have been local
solicitor for the Congested Districts
Board for Co Roscommon for twelve
years. I should mention that I am
inelegible for military service, Yr Obedient Servant Dermot MacDermot The Assistant Under. Secretary. Dublin Castle.
